heading: PreK–12 STEM education
label: 18991 PreK–12 STEM education
notes: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsec. (b) of amended .section 10311 of div. B of Pub. L. 117–167section 10311 of div. B of Pub. L. 117–167section 1862q of this title
amendments: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsec. (b) of amended .section 10311 of div. B of Pub. L. 117–167section 10311 of div. B of Pub. L. 117–167section 1862q of this title

children.text: review the research literature and identify research gaps regarding the interconnected factors that foster and hinder successful implementation of promising, evidence-based PreK–12 STEM education innovations at the local, regional, and national level;
present a compendium of promising, evidence-based PreK–12 STEM education practices, models, programs, and technologies;
identify barriers to widespread and sustained implementation of such innovations; and

make recommendations to the Foundation, the Department of Education, the National Science and Technology Council’s Committee on Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics Education, State and local educational agencies, and other relevant stakeholders on measures to address such barriers.

children.heading: [Log 907 S2522] National STEM Teacher Corps pilot1So in original.1
children.label: c [Log 907 S2522] National STEM Teacher Corps pilot1So in original.1

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 1 Purpose
children.text: It is the purpose of this subsection to elevate the profession of STEM teaching by establishing a National STEM Teacher Corps pilot program to recognize outstanding STEM teachers in our Nation’s classrooms, rewards them for their accomplishments, elevates their public profile, and creates rewarding career paths to which all STEM teachers can aspire, both to prepare future STEM researchers and to create a scientifically literate public.234So in original. Probably should be “reward”.2So in original. Probably should be “elevate”.3So in original. Probably should be “create”.4

children.heading: Administrator
children.label: A Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the National STEM Teacher Corps.

children.label: i
children.text: an institution of higher education; or

children.label: I
children.text: A State educational agency (as defined in ).section 7801 of title 20

children.label: II
children.text: A local educational agency (as defined in ).section 7801 of title 20

children.label: III
children.text: An education nonprofit Association.

children.label: IV
children.text: A cross sector STEM organization.5So in original. Probably should be “cross-sector”.5

children.label: V
children.text: A private entity, including a STEM-related business.

children.heading: High-need school
children.label: C High-need school
children.text: The term “high-need school” has the meaning given the term in .section 6631(b) of title 20

children.heading: Professional development
children.label: D Professional development
children.text: The term “professional Development” has the meaning given the term in .6section 7801 of title 20So in original. Probably should not be capitalized.6

children.heading: Corps Alliance
children.label: E Corps Alliance
children.text: The term “Corps Alliance” means a regionally or topically based award under this subsection.

children.heading: National STEM Teacher Corps Advisory Board
children.label: F National STEM Teacher Corps Advisory Board
children.text: The term “National STEM Teacher Corps Advisory Board” means the Advisory Board for the National STEM Teacher Corps established under paragraph (5).

children.heading: Establishment of National STEM Teacher Corps
children.label: 3 Establishment of National STEM Teacher Corps
children.text: The Director may, subject to the availability of appropriations, establish within the Foundation, a National STEM Teacher Corps 10-year pilot program to be administered by the Administrator, who shall be appointed by the Director. As appropriate, the Director may use existing NSF programs to establish and execute this program.

children.label: I
children.text: deep knowledge of STEM content and pedagogy;

children.label: II
children.text: a passion for STEM subjects and dedication to teaching, evidence of leadership skills, and potential for continued career growth as an educator; and

children.label: III
children.text: demonstrated experience increasing STEM student achievement and STEM participation rates for all students, particularly those from rural and high-need schools; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a uniform selection process, including a comprehensive application that includes recommendations and other relevant professional information;

children.label: B
children.text: promote the National STEM Teacher Corps and elevate best practices that emerge from the National STEM Teacher Corps to a national audience;

children.label: C
children.text: evaluate the operation and effectiveness of the Corps alliances; and

children.label: i
children.text: documenting, monitoring, and assessing the program outcomes or impact on the STEM careers of participants; and

children.label: ii
children.text: documenting, monitoring, and assessing the program outcomes for the STEM education profession nationwide, particularly for rural and high-need schools.

children.heading: National STEM Teacher Corps Advisory Board
children.label: 5 National STEM Teacher Corps Advisory Board

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: There is established a National STEM Teacher Corps Advisory Board to advise the Director on matters pertaining to the National STEM Teacher Corps for the length of the pilot program.

children.label: I
children.text: be appointed by the Director;

children.label: II
children.text: include a representative from each of the following: School leaders, STEM researchers, STEM education researchers, Business leaders, PreK–12 STEM educators, and Students pursuing a postsecondary STEM degree; and

children.label: III
children.text: be geographically diverse.

children.heading: Existing committee
children.label: ii Existing committee
children.text: The Director may assign the duties of the National STEM Teacher Corps Advisory Board to another advisory committee of the Foundation.

children.label: i
children.text: develop and serve the community of National STEM Teacher Corps members within the region or topic area, in coordination with local partners to carry out day-to-day activities;

children.label: ii
children.text: coordinate professional development activities, including activities led by National STEM Teacher Corps members;

children.label: iii
children.text: connect National STEM Teacher Corps members with existing educator professional development programs and coordinate members’ involvement as cooperating teachers or mentors;

children.label: iv
children.text: seek opportunities to involve teachers who are not members of the National STEM Teacher Corps to participate in National STEM Teacher Corps activities; and

children.label: v
children.text: build partnerships with existing education organizations and other efforts by State educational agencies and local educational agencies that operate programs relevant to the National STEM Teacher Corps and its activities;

children.label: B
children.text: recruiting eligible applicants, with a focus on recruiting diverse STEM educators to advance equity based on race, ethnicity, sex, socioeconomic status, age, disability status, geography, and language ability;

children.label: C
children.text: screening, interviewing, and selecting members of the National STEM Teacher Corps using procedures and standards provided by the Administrator;

children.label: D
children.text: coordinating the online network that supports all National STEM Teacher Corps members in the region or topic area;

children.label: E
children.text: convening occasional meetings of National STEM Teacher Corps members in a region or topic area;

children.label: F
children.text: creating opportunities for the professional growth of National STEM Teacher Corps members, with a focus on increasing STEM student achievement and STEM participation rates for all students, particularly those from rural and high-need schools; and

children.label: G
children.text: supporting the retention and success of National STEM Teacher Corps members in the region or topic area.

children.heading: Duties of members of the National STEM Teacher Corps
children.label: 7 Duties of members of the National STEM Teacher Corps
A
children.text: serve a 4-year term with a possibility of reappointment;

children.label: B
children.text: receive an annual stipend in an amount not less than $10,000; and

children.label: i
children.text: working with other members of the National STEM Teacher Corps to develop and improve innovative teaching practices, including practices such as inquiry-based learning;

children.label: ii
children.text: participating in professional development in innovative teaching methodology and mentorship; and

children.label: iii
children.text: continuing to excel in teaching the member’s own students, with a focus on advancing equity by spending additional time teaching and coaching underserved students to increase STEM student achievement and STEM participation rates for students from rural and high-need schools.

children.label: A
children.text: an assessment, drawing on the evaluations the Administrator shall conduct under subparagraphs (C) and (D) of paragraph (4), and other sources of information, of the effectiveness of the pilot program in recruiting and retaining high-quality STEM teachers in the selected regions or topic areas, particularly in high-need and rural schools; and

children.label: B
children.text: if deemed effective, a proposal to Congress for permanent implementation of the pilot program.

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: 9 Sunset
children.text: The authority to carry out this subsection shall terminate on the date that is 15 years after .August 9, 2022

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 10 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $60,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2032 to carry out this subsection.
